outbound_official.note: No single consolidated outbound visa-free list page for Czech ordinary-passport citizens located on any Czech government domain. The Czech MFA (mzv.gov.cz) publishes per-country visa/entry information for Czech travellers in the 'státy světa - informace na cesty' country encyclopedia (https://mzv.gov.cz/jnp/cz/encyklopedie_statu/index.html); each country entry includes a 'Víza - režim vstupu' (Visa - entry regime) subsection. There is no India-style single-page outbound aggregation. The 'cestujeme' travel portal (https://mzv.gov.cz/cestujeme) also provides country-by-country lookup but no consolidated list. Rely on destination-side data and the per-country MFA encyclopedia entries.

visa_free.notes: Biometric passport required. 90 days within any 180-day period. Non-profit stays only.

Citizens of EU, EEA and Schengen member states enjoy freedom of movement and are not subject to visa requirements. This covers all EU member states plus Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland.

visa_policy.notes: Czechia is a Schengen member. Visa-free regime applies only to short-term non-profit stays up to 90 days within any 180-day period. For employment, a Schengen employment visa is required even for visa-free nationalities. The Czech official page lists 195 countries with a visa obligation (list last updated February 9, 2026). No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists; visas are physical stickers issued at embassies/consulates. Eight countries have bilateral Czech-specific visa waiver agreements giving additional stay beyond Schengen 90/180 rule: Argentina, Chile, Israel, South Korea, Costa Rica, Malaysia, Uruguay (each +90 additional days, Czech Republic only) and Singapore (+30 days, up to +60 days in Czech Republic only). Entry/Exit System (EES) launched progressively from October 12, 2025 at Schengen external borders. ETIAS travel authorisation for visa-exempt nationals expected in the last quarter of 2026 (confirmed by Czech Embassy Washington, D.C. - https://mzv.gov.cz/washington/en/consular_information/etias_european_travel_information_and.html). Visa regime for diplomatic/service passport holders may differ. Note: Georgia's diplomatic, service and official passports were suspended from the EU Schengen visa-free regime from March 6, 2026 to March 6, 2027 by European Commission decision - ordinary Georgian passports (biometric) are NOT affected and remain visa-free.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by an EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit in another EU Member State, or EU citizens' family members holding a residence card or permanent residence card in another EU Member State, may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 90 days within any 180-day period (non-profit purposes). Residence permits issued by Ireland and Denmark are excluded. Family members of EU citizens whose short-term visa has expired may also stay up to 3 months if they qualify as spouse, parent, or dependent offspring of an EU citizen.

credential.subtype: long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of scientific research issued by another EU Member State

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of scientific research issued by another EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark) may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 1 year from first entry, provided they are conducting scientific research in Czechia for the same purpose. Must register with Foreign Police within 3 days. Permit/visa must remain valid throughout the stay. Research organisation must notify the Ministry of the Interior. Family members holding a long-term residence permit from another EU Member State may also accompany without a visa.

credential.subtype: long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of studies (exchange programme) issued by another EU Member State

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid long-term visa or long-term residence permit for the purpose of studies issued by another EU Member State (excluding Ireland and Denmark), where the permit was issued under an exchange programme including mobility programmes (e.g. Erasmus) or under agreements between universities (the permit must contain a note about the exchange programme), may stay in the Czech Republic without a visa for up to 1 year from first entry, provided they are studying at a university in Czechia. Must register with Foreign Police within 3 days. Permit/visa must remain valid throughout the stay. University must notify the Ministry of the Interior.

Holders of diplomatic, service, official or special passports from countries that have concluded bilateral visa waiver agreements with the Czech Republic are exempt from the visa requirement for short stays. The specific countries and maximum stay periods are governed by individual bilateral treaties. The visa regime for holders of diplomatic, service and special passports may differ from ordinary passport holders - holders are advised to contact the respective Czech consulate to confirm. The 'Countries with Visa Waiver Regime' page (mzv.gov.cz) lists these bilateral agreements but the full per-country list could not be rendered due to JavaScript gating. Note: Georgian diplomatic, service and official passports are currently excluded from the Schengen visa-free regime by European Commission decision (suspended March 6, 2026 – March 6, 2027); Georgian ordinary passport holders with biometric passports are unaffected and remain visa-free.

cbi.notes: Czech Republic does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is available through naturalization after qualifying residency periods, or by descent. No official government source confirms any direct investment-for-citizenship pathway. Confirmed via ipc.gov.cz and mzv.gov.cz.

programs.name: Long-Term Residence Permit for the Purpose of Investing
programs.notes: Minimum investment of CZK 75 million (confirmed current as of 2026). Must create at least 20 new jobs for EU citizens or their family members for a minimum of 2 years. Up to 60% of financial investment may be satisfied through tangible or intangible assets (e.g., technologies, know-how). Permit valid for up to 2 years, renewable for up to 2 years at a time. Processing: 30 days standard, 60 days for complex cases. Administrative fee: 2,500 CZK (in-country) or 5,000 CZK (from abroad). Available to businesspeople (natural persons) or partners/members of statutory bodies/authorised representatives of commercial corporations. Also accessible via czechinvest.gov.cz.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Program
programs.eligibility: Highly qualified IT and marketing professionals. Two categories: (1) employees of a foreign company working remotely in Czechia who remain employed abroad; (2) freelancers holding Czech business license in IT or marketing. Available to nationals of: Australia, Brazil, India, Israel, Japan, Canada, South Korea, Mexico, New Zealand, Singapore, UK, USA, and Taiwan. Applicants must not have held a valid Czech long-term visa or residence permit within the preceding year.
programs.notes: Established by Czech Government Decree No. 475 of June 28, 2023. Confirmed active as of July 2025 per MPO website. Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade (digitalnomad@mpo.gov.cz). Family members (spouse, registered partner, children) may apply simultaneously. Inclusion valid for maximum 1 year. No legal entitlement to participation. Long-term visa or residence permit issued after approval.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Worker Programme
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals in positions classified as CZ-ISCO main classes 1-3 (managerial, professional, technical). Employer must have operated in Czech Republic for at least 2 years, settled financial obligations, and employ minimum 3 people. Salary must meet average income for relevant CZ-ISCO sub-group or Blue Card minimum. Sectors: manufacturing, services, public sector (excluding medical).
programs.notes: Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade (vysocekvalifikovani@mpo.gov.cz) or Ministry of Health for healthcare (programzdravotnictvi@mzcr.cz). Inclusion valid for 1 year. Enables priority employee card application at Czech embassies/consulates abroad. Family members included. No legal entitlement to participation. Contact for investors: afc-visa@czechinvest.gov.cz.

programs.name: Key and Research Staff Programme
programs.eligibility: Statutory representatives, managers and key specialists of significant Czech and foreign investors, research organisations, technological companies and start-ups. Employees in intra-corporate transfer (managerial/specialist) or newly hired in CZ-ISCO classes 1-3. Workers from all third countries.
programs.notes: Administered by Ministry of Industry and Trade. Programme modified June 1, 2026 - expanded provisions for Ukrainian citizens with specific residency and documentation requirements. Contact: klicovypersonal@mpo.gov.cz (investors), programklicovy@czechinvest.org (research/tech/start-ups). Inclusion valid for 1 year; subsequent additions require only application form and salary declaration. Benefits include 30-day visa processing, priority submission at Czech embassies abroad, family member inclusion, and simplified documentation.

programs.name: EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with completed higher vocational or university education of at least 3 years. Must work in a position requiring high qualifications (CZ-ISCO 1-3) with a work contract of at least 1 year. Salary must be at least 1.5 times the average gross salary in the Czech Republic.
programs.notes: Long-term residence permit enabling third-country citizens to stay and work in Czechia for more than 3 months in a highly qualified position. Administered by Ministry of the Interior. Also documented at ipc.gov.cz and mv.gov.cz.

validation_notes: Updated notes for 8 countries with bilateral Czech-specific visa waiver agreements to reflect additional stay beyond Schengen 90/180 rule: Argentina, Chile, Israel, South Korea, Costa Rica, Malaysia, Uruguay (+90 additional days each in CZ only) and Singapore (+30 days, up to +60 days in CZ only). Previous record did not capture this distinction.

Confirmed RBI minimum investment of CZK 75 million remains unchanged; no threshold update found.
Digital Nomad Program confirmed active as of July 2025 per MPO official site; no eligibility changes found.

Georgia ordinary passport (biometric) remains visa-free - no change to visa_free list entry for Georgia.

IPC visa-free-stays page re-confirmed; conditional_access entries (EU residence permit, research, study) verified unchanged. RBI minimum investment CZK 75 million confirmed unchanged at ipc.gov.cz. Digital Nomad Program still active at mpo.gov.cz with same 13 eligible nationalities. ETIAS timeline remains last quarter of 2026 per mzv.gov.cz/washington. No data changes required; record remains accurate.

Diplomatic/service/official/special passport waiver country-by-country list: The 'Countries with Visa Waiver Regime' page (mzv.gov.cz) is JavaScript-gated and full list could not be retrieved. Only bilateral ordinary-passport agreement countries are confirmed.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Tourism
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to visit Czechia and the Schengen Area for tourism, recreational travel, and sightseeing for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Standard Schengen visa fee is €80 (approx. USD 99 at mid-2026 rates); reduced fee of €35 for certain nationals (Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Bosnia, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine). Children aged 6–11 pay €40; under-6 free. Visa is a sticker affixed to the passport; no e-visa system. Single or multiple entries available depending on application. Valid for up to 90 days within any 180-day window across the entire Schengen Area.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Business
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to travel to Czechia and the Schengen Area for business meetings, conferences, trade fairs, and similar commercial activities for short stays up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Same fee structure as tourist Schengen visa (€80 standard, €35 reduced). Does not authorise employment. Purpose-specific supporting documents required (e.g. invitation letter from Czech company). Multiple-entry visas may be issued for frequent business travellers.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Required for nationals of specified countries to pass through the international transit zone of Czech airports without entering the Schengen Area territory.
visa_types.notes: Required for citizens of ~29 countries including Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Bangladesh, Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Russia, Syria, Turkey, Yemen. Can be issued for a maximum of six months validity with one or two entries. Applicant must demonstrate onward journey to final destination outside Schengen. Exempt: holders of valid Schengen visa, residence permits issued by Canada, Japan, or the USA, and diplomatic passport holders. Fee same as Type C (€80 standard).

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa / Residence Permit for Study (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Grants third-country nationals the right to reside in Czechia for the purpose of study at an accredited Czech educational institution for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: The initial visa is a single-entry 60-day visa issued to collect the residence card from the Ministry of Interior. Processing time is 60 days. Fee is 2,500 CZK (~USD 115) for the visa plus 1,000 CZK (~USD 46) for the residence card; total approx. USD 160. Covers university study, language preparation, secondary school exchanges, Erasmus-equivalent programmes, non-paid internships, and European Voluntary Service. Requires acceptance letter, proof of financial means, accommodation proof, criminal record extract. Card validity matches study programme duration. Holders may travel in Schengen for up to 90 days per 180 days for non-work purposes.

visa_types.name: Employee Card (Zaměstnanecká karta)
visa_types.purpose: Combined residence and work permit for third-country nationals employed in the Czech Republic for more than three months, valid for the duration of the employment contract.
visa_types.notes: Requires at least 15 hours of work per week and minimum wage. Initial single-entry 60-day visa issued to collect the Employee Card from the Ministry of Interior. Processing time 60 days standard, extendable to 90 days if Labour Office binding opinion required. Application fee: 5,000 CZK (~USD 217). Requires vacancy reference number from the Czech Labour Office central vacancies database. Incomplete applications are suspended until all documents provided. Holder gains Schengen Area travel rights of 90 days per 180 days for non-work purposes.

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card (Modrá karta EU)
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for highly qualified third-country nationals working in a position requiring high qualifications in the Czech Republic for more than three months.
visa_types.notes: Requires university degree or higher professional education of at least 3 years. Employment contract must be for at least 1 year with at least 40 hours per week. Salary minimum: 1.5x the average gross annual salary in Czechia (currently ~382,620 CZK per year). Processing: 90 days. Fee: 5,000 CZK (~USD 217). Initial visa is single-entry 60-day to collect the Blue Card. Card is renewable while employment continues. Grants same Schengen mobility rights as Employee Card.

visa_types.name: Intra-Company Transfer Card (ICT Card)
visa_types.purpose: Permits managers, specialists, and trainee employees of multinational companies to be transferred to a branch or subsidiary in the Czech Republic for more than three months.
visa_types.notes: Covers managers, specialists, and interns transferred within the same company or corporate group. Maximum stay: 3 years for managers/specialists; 1 year for trainees. Fee: 5,000 CZK (~USD 217). Processing time: 90 days standard; 120 days for complex cases. Initial single-entry 60-day visa issued to collect the card. Must demonstrate the intra-corporate transfer and that the applicant has been employed by the sending entity for at least 3–12 months (category-dependent).

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa for Seasonal Employment (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to work in seasonal sectors in the Czech Republic for periods exceeding 90 days up to a maximum of nine months.
visa_types.notes: Visa valid for a maximum of 9 months; actual seasonal employment cannot exceed 6 months. Restricted to 5 sectors: crop and animal farming, forestry, accommodation services, restaurant services, and sports/entertainment/recreational services. Requires employment permit from the Czech Labour Office. Fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109). Processing: 60 days. Requires proof of accommodation, financial means, and criminal record extract.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa for Entrepreneurship (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to operate a business, work as self-employed, or serve as statutory representatives of companies in the Czech Republic for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Covers self-employment, sole traders (živnostník), and statutory organs or members of commercial corporations. Maximum validity: 1 year; extendable or convertible to a residence permit. Processing: 90 days standard, up to 120 days for complex cases. Fee: 5,000 CZK (~USD 217). Requires trade licence or company register extract, proof of accommodation, proof of financial means, criminal record extract. All documents must be in Czech or officially certified translation and not older than 180 days. Application must be submitted in person at a Czech consulate abroad.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa for Training / Internship (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to undertake vocational training, professional development, or internship programmes in the Czech Republic for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Covers skill and qualification enhancement programmes exceeding 3 months. Fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109). Processing: 90 days, extendable to 120 days. Requires documentation of the training programme, accommodation, and financial means. Not for gainful employment.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa for Scientific Research (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Allows researchers, scientists, and visiting professors from third countries to conduct scientific research or academic work at a Czech research institution for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Must be hosted by an accredited Czech research organisation. Processing: 90 days. Fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109). Holders may travel in other Schengen states for up to 90 days per 180 days for non-gainful purposes. Researchers may also benefit from visa-free stays in Czechia if they hold a long-term residence permit for research from another EU Member State (see conditional_access entries).

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa / Residence Permit for Family Reunification (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Permits family members of third-country nationals legally residing in Czechia to join and reside with them for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: English-language details on the MFA page are 'under preparation'; the Ministry of Interior (ipc.gov.cz) provides additional details. Fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109) for long-term visa stage. Eligible family members include spouse/partner, minor children, and dependent relatives. Processing: 90 days standard, up to 120 days for complex cases. Sponsor must demonstrate sufficient income and accommodation. Family members of EU citizens holding a residence card in another EU Member State may enter visa-free for up to 90 days (see conditional_access).

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa for Health / Culture / Sports / Official Purposes (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Covers stays exceeding 90 days in Czechia for medical treatment, cultural activities, sports participation, or official and political missions not falling under other specific categories.
visa_types.notes: General long-term visa category for purposes not covered by specialised categories. Maximum validity: 1 year. Fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109). Processing: 90 days, extendable to 120 days. Can be converted to or extended as a residence permit before expiry. Holders gain Schengen Area travel rights of 90 days per 180 days for non-gainful purposes.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Medical / Spa Treatment
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to visit Czechia for medical treatment, hospital stays, rehabilitation, or spa/wellness treatment for short stays up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Type C Schengen visa. Requires medical documentation (e.g. appointment confirmation from Czech medical facility). Fee: €80 standard (~USD 99); students and researchers may qualify for fee exemption. For longer medical stays, the Long-Stay Visa for health purposes (Type D) applies.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Enables young nationals of bilateral partner countries to combine travel and temporary employment in Czechia for an extended stay, typically up to 1 year.
visa_types.notes: Czech MFA English-language page for Working Holiday notes details are only available in Czech. Programme exists under bilateral agreements with select countries. Typically open to applicants aged 18–30 or 18–35 depending on bilateral treaty. Fee likely 2,500 CZK (~USD 109) based on long-term visa fee schedule. Contact wwwadmin@mzv.cz or nearest Czech embassy for country-specific eligibility and details.

visa_types.name: Long-Term Residence Permit for Investment
visa_types.purpose: Grants third-country nationals who make a qualifying investment in Czechia of at least CZK 75 million and create at least 20 jobs the right to reside in the Czech Republic.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment: CZK 75 million. Must create at least 20 new jobs for EU citizens or their family members for a minimum of 2 years. Up to 60% of investment may be in tangible/intangible assets (e.g. technologies, know-how). Permit valid for up to 2 years, renewable for 2-year periods. Administrative fee: 2,500 CZK (~USD 109) if applied in-country; 5,000 CZK (~USD 217) if applied from abroad. Available to natural persons or partners/statutory body members of commercial corporations. Also accessible via czechinvest.gov.cz.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Program (Long-Stay Visa or Residence Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Enables highly qualified IT and marketing professionals from eligible countries to live in Czechia while working remotely for a foreign employer or as a Czech-licensed freelancer.
visa_types.notes: Established by Czech Government Decree No. 475 (June 28, 2023). Eligible nationalities: Australia, Brazil, India, Israel, Japan, Canada, South Korea, Mexico, New Zealand, Singapore, UK, USA, and Taiwan. Two categories: (1) remote employees of a foreign company; (2) freelancers with Czech business licence in IT or marketing. Applicants must not have held a valid Czech long-term visa/residence permit in the preceding year. Inclusion decision valid for up to 1 year. Family members may apply simultaneously. Processing: 45 days. Administered by Czech Ministry of Industry and Trade (digitalnomad@mpo.gov.cz). No legal entitlement to programme participation.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Visa Waiver (bilateral treaty-based)
visa_types.purpose: Exempts holders of diplomatic, service, official, or special passports from countries with bilateral visa waiver agreements with Czechia from the visa requirement for short stays.
visa_types.notes: The list of countries whose diplomatic/service/official/special passport holders are exempt is published on the MFA page (JavaScript-gated; full list not retrievable). Stay periods are governed by individual bilateral treaties and may differ from the standard Schengen 90/180 rule. Holders are advised to contact the nearest Czech consulate to confirm their specific visa regime before travel.
